executiveSummary: Today in ML, three threads pull on the same knot: interpretability is converging on attention-head-level analysis, a disagreement is emerging about whether heads are the right unit at all, and two papers offer competing ablation strategies.

themes.title: Interpretability converges on attention heads
themes.argument: Both papers analyze circuits at the attention-head level, using different ablation strategies but reaching compatible conclusions about head specialization.

papers.title: Circuit-level analysis of reasoning
papers.onelinePitch: A mechanistic account of how attention heads compose into multi-step reasoning circuits.
papers.whyMatters: Directly grounded in your stated interest in mechanistic interpretability.

papers.title: Head pruning ablations
papers.onelinePitch: Ablation evidence that only a small subset of attention heads matter for task-specific reasoning.
papers.whyMatters: Tests the framing from your March 3 starred paper on circuit sparsity.

debates.title: Are attention heads the right unit?
debates.summary: Smith argues attention-head-level analysis is sufficient; Chen counters that activation patches are more reliable.

proactiveQuestions.question: You've starred 3 papers on normalizing flows this week. Should I weight flow-based methods higher in scoring, or is this a temporary interest?
